Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/I quit it up|I quit it up]] ([[User talk:I quit it up|talk]]): Unexplained removal

Atoms are extremely small, typically around 100 picometers across. A human hair is about a million carbon atoms wide. Atoms are smaller than the shortest wavelength of visible light, which means humans cannot see atoms with conventional microscopes. They are so small that accurately predicting their behavior using classical physics is not possible due to quantum effects.  More than 99.94% of an atom's mass is in the nucleus. Protons have a positive electric charge and neutrons have no charge,

/* Further reading */ Interesting book about this history of scientific thought and progress thereof, but not really about atoms. The progress of *atom* understanding is already covered with appropriate cites.

/* Further reading */ all about quantum theory, which is already covered in sufficient detail and with cites for the main "atom" article. The extensive quantum/particle-physics/etc beyond that has its own other articles.
/* Further reading */ more pruning (see my prev ES)
/* Further reading */ [[CRC]] is a great ref about chemical properties, but not really substantive content on-topic for the main article about "atom" (rather than substances containing them)

/* Further reading */ Greek–English lexicon could be used as primary cite for the original Greek, but we already have secondary sources about the whole idea used as cites where we discuss these Greek terms
remove |ref=harv (per [[:Category:CS1 maint: ref=harv]], this is the default now)
/* Valence and bonding behavior */ more science-authoritative def (and full ref for it)
/* Energy levels */ rewording remainder to match my previous comment about potential energy being a variable on an affine scale
/* Energy levels */ rm pointless parenthesis (and indeed nonsense, since potential energy is an affine quantity – there is no zero on the scale, other than by convention)

/* Atoms in philosophy */ don't repeat the article name in headings
